design_philosophy.core_loop: Select anthropomorphic animal fighter → learn and customize special moves via password system → compete in tournament bracket → challenge final boss Dali Llama for Belt of Heaven
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning and mastering a unique move set for each character while adapting to opponent patterns across tournament stages
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become the world's greatest martial artist by mastering animal-themed fighting styles and earning the right to challenge the champion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasis on move customization and learning system (password-based move storage) as core progression mechanic rather than unlocking. This positions the game as character-development-focused rather than purely execution-based. The anthropomorphic animal roster with distinct martial arts archetypes (Thai boxer rat, Soviet bear commando, politician vixen, cult member panther) reflects quirky thematic commitment to personality-driven fighter design.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through move customization system. Players can learn new attacks and save them via passwords, allowing experimentation with move sets between matches. This creates tunable difficulty based on player investment in learning optimal move combinations.

progression.upgrade_system: Move learning system: players acquire new special attacks through gameplay and can save custom move sets using passwords. Each character has learnable special moves that are retained across sessions.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Secret button sequences unlock hidden characters (Karate Croc, Dali Llama). Brutal Unleashed adds two new characters (Chung Poe, Psycho Kitty) and new arenas in the sequel tournament.
replayability_hooks: Password-based move customization encourages experimentation with different fighting styles
Multiple playable characters with distinct martial arts archetypes
Hidden characters accessible via secret inputs
Tournament structure with multiple opponents per playthrough

world_building_techniques: Character archetypes with distinct professions/backgrounds (rock star lion, politician vixen, ex-Soviet bear commando, cult member panther)
Tournament framing device occurring every four years on 'uncharted Brutal Island'
Dali Llama positioned as undefeated champion and ultimate test of 'warrior's spirit'
Inter-character relationships hinted at (Prince Leon's vendetta with Tai Cheetah and Kendo Coyote; mentor-pupil dynamic between Tai Cheetah and Kendo Coyote)

boss_design.philosophy: Dali Llama serves as final tournament boss representing the pinnacle of martial mastery and the ultimate test of the player's learned move set. The tournament structure implies progressive difficulty scaling through opponent variety.
notable_bosses: Dali Llama - Belt of Heaven championship holder, undefeated champion representing the ultimate challenge

faction_indicators: The Pantha - member of a sinister cult
Tai Cheetah - mentoring role suggesting school/lineage affiliation
Ivan the Bear - ex-Soviet commando background suggests military faction identity
danger_signals: The Pantha's cult membership indicates organized threat
Kendo Coyote's greed motivation suggests mercenary or corrupting influence
Prince Leon's vendetta with multiple fighters suggests escalating conflict
Rhei Rat's arrogance combined with Thai boxer expertise indicates aggressive dominance display
environmental_storytelling: Anthropomorphic animal rosters allow instant visual communication of fighter archetype and fighting style philosophy
Tournament island setting establishes neutral ground where diverse martial traditions converge
Distinct character archetypes (politician, rock star, bar brawler) suggest cross-faction recruitment to tournament
